always.exam: Ottawa Ankle Rules screen (bony tenderness posterior malleoli/navicular/5th MT base, ability to weight bear 4 steps)
always.why: A validated, high-sensitivity rule to exclude fracture before proceeding with soft-tissue special testing.
always.confirmsOrExcludes: Screens for occult ankle/foot fracture requiring imaging referral

always.exam: Active + passive ankle ROM (dorsiflexion, plantarflexion, inversion, eversion) with end-feel
always.why: Establishes the mobility baseline and end-feel pattern that differentiates capsular/OA restriction from soft-tissue/ligamentous presentations.
always.confirmsOrExcludes: Screens mechanical restriction pattern vs ligamentous laxity

always.exam: Ligament stability screen (anterior drawer, talar tilt, squeeze/mortise test)
always.why: The three core stability tests differentiate lateral (ATFL/CFL) sprain from high (syndesmotic) sprain and quantify laxity grade.
always.confirmsOrExcludes: Confirms/excludes ATFL/CFL laxity vs syndesmotic injury

add.exam: Thompson's test + Royal London Hospital test + Achilles palpation
add.why: Achilles pain requires urgently excluding complete rupture (Thompson's) before proceeding to confirm tendinopathy (Royal London).
add.confirmsOrExcludes: Excludes Achilles rupture; confirms mid-portion tendinopathy

add.exam: Thompson's test + insertional palpation + single-leg heel raise endurance
add.why: Insertional Achilles pain needs rupture excluded and load tolerance quantified to guide eccentric loading dosage.
add.confirmsOrExcludes: Excludes rupture; confirms insertional tendinopathy severity

add.exam: Thompson's test + palpable tendon gap check, before any weight-bearing testing
add.why: A flagged suspected rupture must be confirmed or excluded before any further loading or resisted testing is attempted.

add.exam: Talar tilt test + single-leg balance/proprioception screen + subtalar mobility assessment
add.why: Reported instability needs both mechanical laxity and functional/proprioceptive deficits assessed, since chronic instability has both components.
add.confirmsOrExcludes: Confirms/excludes chronic ankle instability (mechanical vs functional)

add.exam: Navicular drop test + resisted inversion in plantarflexion + single-leg heel raise (too-many-toes sign)
add.why: Medial ankle pain in an adult raises suspicion for tibialis posterior dysfunction / progressive flatfoot, which is easily missed without a dedicated arch assessment.

add.exam: Tinel's sign at tarsal tunnel + plantar foot sensory mapping
add.why: A neuropathic-sounding referral pattern requires differentiating tarsal tunnel syndrome from a local musculoskeletal source.

add.exam: Point bony palpation + hop test + training-load history, before any further loaded testing
add.why: A suspected stress fracture requires screening before continuing with provocative/resisted testing that could aggravate it.
add.confirmsOrExcludes: Screens for stress fracture requiring imaging referral before continued loading

supporting.exam: Resisted testing (dorsiflexion, plantarflexion, inversion, eversion) for strength and pain-on-resist
supporting.why: Differentiates contractile (tendon/muscle) sources from inert (ligament/capsule) sources per Cyriax selective tissue tension principles.
supporting.confirmsOrExcludes: Localises contractile vs inert tissue involvement

supporting.exam: Kinetic chain screen: weight-bearing dorsiflexion lunge test + subtalar mobility
supporting.why: Ankle mobility deficits are a well-established driver of compensatory knee, hip, and lumbar loading patterns further up the chain.
supporting.confirmsOrExcludes: Screens ankle mobility contribution to the broader kinetic chain

supporting.exam: Functional + outcome measure (single-leg balance, hop tests, PSFS top 3 activities)
supporting.why: Quantifies baseline function and instability risk for goal-setting, return-to-sport planning, and re-assessment.
supporting.confirmsOrExcludes: Baseline functional severity and re-injury risk

supporting.exam: Imaging (only if Ottawa Rules positive, suspected rupture, or failed conservative management)
supporting.why: Imaging is not routine for straightforward soft-tissue ankle sprains; reserve for fracture screening, suspected rupture, or non-resolving presentations.
supporting.confirmsOrExcludes: Confirms structural pathology when clinically indicated

assessmentLayers.Observation: Swelling/bruising pattern, deformity, foot posture, antalgic gait
assessmentLayers.Posture: Foot pronation / supination, navicular drop, calcaneal alignment
assessmentLayers.Functional screen: Single-leg balance / Star-excursion, single-leg heel raises, hop, weight-bearing lunge
assessmentLayers.STTT (Cyriax): Resisted dorsiflexion / plantarflexion / inversion / eversion — contractile source
assessmentLayers.CPA (compensation pattern): Peroneal / tibialis-posterior inhibition vs gastrocnemius overactivity
assessmentLayers.Kinetic chain: Weight-bearing dorsiflexion (lunge) & subtalar mobility as chain drivers; knee/hip above
assessmentLayers.Fascia: Superficial & deep back line (gastroc-soleus-plantar continuity), spiral line
